records.title: Cryo-EM structure of the endogenous U2/branchpoint spliceosomal complex (Proximal DHX15 state)
records.paper_title: The tumour suppressor RBM5 activates the helicase DHX15 to regulate splicing.
records.authors: Liu S; Su T; Huang J; Lin CH; Black DL; Damianov A; Zhou ZH
records.authors_short: Liu S; Su T; Huang J; et al. (7 authors)
records.abstract: Pre-mRNA splicing determines the expressed proteome and is frequently dysregulated in cancer. The tumour-suppressor RBM5 controls an exon network regulating apoptosis, yet its molecular mechanism is elusive. Using in vivo spliceosome capture and cryogenic electron microscopy, we determined structures of precatalytic spliceosomes arrested by RBM5 immediately after U2 snRNP branchpoint recognition. Despite intron diversity, the U2-pre-mRNA duplex, branchpoint adenine, and downstream polypyrimidine tract are well-resolved. RBM5 binds the outer SF3B1 HEAT surface and performs dual functions: First, its helix-loop-helix motif and upstream zinc-finger domain sterically block tri-snRNP and Prp8 docking and prevent progression to pre-B and Bact complexes; Second, its G-patch activates DHX15 and places this DExH-box helicase on the pre-mRNA as it exits SF3B1, poised for branch helix unwinding. DHX15 binding to SF3B1 is facilitated by U2SURP/SR140, which engages SF3B1 near RBM5's helix-loop-helix. Functional assays confirm that disruption of the RBM5 interfaces with either DHX15 or SF3B1 inhibit exon repression. Mutations at these regulatory interfaces are common in cancer genomes and predicted to disrupt its regulation of apoptotic isoforms. Thus, RBM5 acts as a dual-action spliceosome gatekeeper that couples helicase activation with physical stalling to enforce tumour-suppressive alternative splicing programmes.

records.paper_title: The intrinsically disordered TSSC4 protein acts as a helicase inhibitor, placeholder and multi-interaction coordinator during snRNP assembly and recycling.
records.authors: Bergfort A; Hilal T; Kuropka B; Ilik İA; Weber G; Aktaş T; Freund C; Wahl MC
records.authors_short: Bergfort A; Hilal T; Kuropka B; et al. (8 authors)
records.abstract: Biogenesis of spliceosomal small nuclear ribonucleoproteins (snRNPs) and their recycling after splicing require numerous assembly/recycling factors whose modes of action are often poorly understood. The intrinsically disordered TSSC4 protein has been identified as a nuclear-localized U5 snRNP and U4/U6-U5 tri-snRNP assembly/recycling factor, but how TSSC4's intrinsic disorder supports TSSC4 functions remains unknown. Using diverse interaction assays and cryogenic electron microscopy-based structural analysis, we show that TSSC4 employs four conserved, non-contiguous regions to bind the PRPF8 Jab1/MPN domain and the SNRNP200 helicase at functionally important sites. It thereby inhibits SNRNP200 helicase activity, spatially aligns the proteins, coordinates formation of a U5 sub-module and transiently blocks premature interaction of SNRNP200 with at least three other spliceosomal factors. Guided by the structure, we designed a TSSC4 variant that lacks stable binding to the PRPF8 Jab1/MPN domain or SNRNP200 in vitro. Comparative immunoprecipitation/mass spectrometry from HEK293 nuclear extract revealed distinct interaction profiles of wild type TSSC4 and the variant deficient in PRPF8/SNRNP200 binding with snRNP proteins, other spliceosomal proteins as well as snRNP assembly/recycling factors and chaperones. Our findings elucidate molecular strategies employed by an intrinsically disordered protein to promote snRNP assembly, and suggest multiple TSSC4-dependent stages during snRNP assembly/recycling.

records.title: Structure of the recycling U5 snRNP bound to chaperones CD2BP2 and TSSC4 (State 2)
records.paper_title: Structural basis of human U5 snRNP late biogenesis and recycling.
records.authors: Riabov Bassat D; Visanpattanasin S; Vorländer MK; Fin L; Phillips AW; Plaschka C
records.authors_short: Riabov Bassat D; Visanpattanasin S; Vorländer MK; et al. (6 authors)
records.abstract: Pre-mRNA splicing by the spliceosome requires the biogenesis and recycling of its small nuclear ribonucleoprotein (snRNP) complexes, which are consumed in each round of splicing. The human U5 snRNP is the ~1 MDa 'heart' of the spliceosome and is recycled through an unknown mechanism involving major architectural rearrangements and the dedicated chaperones CD2BP2 and TSSC4. Late steps in U5 snRNP biogenesis similarly involve these chaperones. Here we report cryo-electron microscopy structures of four human U5 snRNP-CD2BP2-TSSC4 complexes, revealing how a series of molecular events primes the U5 snRNP to generate the ~2 MDa U4/U6.U5 tri-snRNP, the largest building block of the spliceosome.

records.title: Cryo-EM structure of the spliceosomal U4/U6.U5 tri-snRNP
records.paper_title: The 3.8 Å structure of the U4/U6.U5 tri-snRNP: Insights into spliceosome assembly and catalysis.
records.authors: Wan R; Yan C; Bai R; Wang L; Huang M; Wong CC; Shi Y
records.authors_short: Wan R; Yan C; Bai R; et al. (7 authors)
records.abstract: Splicing of precursor messenger RNA is accomplished by a dynamic megacomplex known as the spliceosome. Assembly of a functional spliceosome requires a preassembled U4/U6.U5 tri-snRNP complex, which comprises the U5 small nuclear ribonucleoprotein (snRNP), the U4 and U6 small nuclear RNA (snRNA) duplex, and a number of protein factors. Here we report the three-dimensional structure of a Saccharomyces cerevisiae U4/U6.U5 tri-snRNP at an overall resolution of 3.8 angstroms by single-particle electron cryomicroscopy. The local resolution for the core regions of the tri-snRNP reaches 3.0 to 3.5 angstroms, allowing construction of a refined atomic model. Our structure contains U5 snRNA, the extensively base-paired U4/U6 snRNA, and 30 proteins including Prp8 and Snu114, which amount to 8495 amino acids and 263 nucleotides with a combined molecular mass of ~1 megadalton. The catalytic nucleotide U80 from U6 snRNA exists in an inactive conformation, stabilized by its base-pairing interactions with U4 snRNA and protected by Prp3. Pre-messenger RNA is bound in the tri-snRNP through base-pairing interactions with U6 snRNA and loop I of U5 snRNA. This structure, together with that of the spliceosome, reveals the molecular choreography of the snRNAs in the activation process of the spliceosomal ribozyme.

records.title: 3D structure determination of the human*U4/U6.U5* tri-snRNP complex
records.paper_title: Molecular architecture of the human U4/U6.U5 tri-snRNP.
records.authors: Agafonov DE; Kastner B; Dybkov O; Hofele RV; Liu WT; Urlaub H; Lührmann R; Stark H
records.authors_short: Agafonov DE; Kastner B; Dybkov O; et al. (8 authors)
records.abstract: The U4/U6.U5 triple small nuclear ribonucleoprotein (tri-snRNP) is a major spliceosome building block. We obtained a three-dimensional structure of the 1.8-megadalton human tri-snRNP at a resolution of 7 angstroms using single-particle cryo-electron microscopy (cryo-EM). We fit all known high-resolution structures of tri-snRNP components into the EM density map and validated them by protein cross-linking. Our model reveals how the spatial organization of Brr2 RNA helicase prevents premature U4/U6 RNA unwinding in isolated human tri-snRNPs and how the ubiquitin C-terminal hydrolase-like protein Sad1 likely tethers the helicase Brr2 to its preactivation position. Comparison of our model with cryo-EM three-dimensional structures of the Saccharomyces cerevisiae tri-snRNP and Schizosaccharomyces pombe spliceosome indicates that Brr2 undergoes a marked conformational change during spliceosome activation, and that the scaffolding protein Prp8 is also rearranged to accommodate the spliceosome's catalytic RNA network.

records.title: Foot region of the yeast spliceosomal U4/U6.U5 tri-snRNP
records.paper_title: Cryo-EM structure of the yeast U4/U6.U5 tri-snRNP at 3.7 Å resolution.
records.authors: Nguyen THD; Galej WP; Bai XC; Oubridge C; Newman AJ; Scheres SHW; Nagai K
records.authors_short: Nguyen THD; Galej WP; Bai XC; et al. (7 authors)
records.abstract: U4/U6.U5 tri-snRNP represents a substantial part of the spliceosome before activation. A cryo-electron microscopy structure of Saccharomyces cerevisiae U4/U6.U5 tri-snRNP at 3.7 Å resolution led to an essentially complete atomic model comprising 30 proteins plus U4/U6 and U5 small nuclear RNAs (snRNAs). The structure reveals striking interweaving interactions of the protein and RNA components, including extended polypeptides penetrating into subunit interfaces. The invariant ACAGAGA sequence of U6 snRNA, which base-pairs with the 5'-splice site during catalytic activation, forms a hairpin stabilized by Dib1 and Prp8 while the adjacent nucleotides interact with the exon binding loop 1 of U5 snRNA. Snu114 harbours GTP, but its putative catalytic histidine is held away from the γ-phosphate by hydrogen bonding to a tyrosine in the amino-terminal domain of Prp8. Mutation of this histidine to alanine has no detectable effect on yeast growth. The structure provides important new insights into the spliceosome activation process leading to the formation of the catalytic centre.

records.title: Structure of the human U5.U4/U6 tri-snRNP at 2.9A resolution.
records.paper_title: Mechanism of 5' splice site transfer for human spliceosome activation.
records.authors: Charenton C; Wilkinson ME; Nagai K
records.authors_short: Charenton C; Wilkinson ME; Nagai K
records.abstract: The prespliceosome, comprising U1 and U2 small nuclear ribonucleoproteins (snRNPs) bound to the precursor messenger RNA 5' splice site (5'SS) and branch point sequence, associates with the U4/U6.U5 tri-snRNP to form the fully assembled precatalytic pre-B spliceosome. Here, we report cryo-electron microscopy structures of the human pre-B complex captured before U1 snRNP dissociation at 3.3-angstrom core resolution and the human tri-snRNP at 2.9-angstrom resolution. U1 snRNP inserts the 5'SS-U1 snRNA helix between the two RecA domains of the Prp28 DEAD-box helicase. Adenosine 5'-triphosphate-dependent closure of the Prp28 RecA domains releases the 5'SS to pair with the nearby U6 ACAGAGA-box sequence presented as a mobile loop. The structures suggest that formation of the 5'SS-ACAGAGA helix triggers remodeling of an intricate protein-RNA network to induce Brr2 helicase relocation to its loading sequence in U4 snRNA, enabling Brr2 to unwind the U4/U6 snRNA duplex to allow U6 snRNA to form the catalytic center of the spliceosome.

records.title: Structure of the human 20S U5 snRNP core
records.paper_title: Structure of the human 20S U5 snRNP.
records.authors: Schneider S; Brandina I; Peter D; Lagad S; Fraudeau A; Portell-Montserrat J; Tholen J; Zhao J; Galej WP
records.authors_short: Schneider S; Brandina I; Peter D; et al. (9 authors)
records.abstract: The 20S U5 small nuclear ribonucleoprotein particle (snRNP) is a 17-subunit RNA-protein complex and a precursor of the U4/U6.U5 tri-snRNP, the major building block of the precatalytic spliceosome. CD2BP2 is a hallmark protein of the 20S U5 snRNP, absent from the mature tri-snRNP. Here we report a high-resolution cryogenic electron microscopy structure of the 20S U5 snRNP, shedding light on the mutually exclusive interfaces utilized during tri-snRNP assembly and the role of the CD2BP2 in facilitating this process.

records.title: Cryo-EM structure of the yeast pre-B complex at an average resolution of 3.4~4.6 angstrom (tri-snRNP and U2 snRNP Part)
records.paper_title: Structures of the fully assembled Saccharomyces cerevisiae spliceosome before activation.
records.authors: Bai R; Wan R; Yan C; Lei J; Shi Y
records.authors_short: Bai R; Wan R; Yan C; et al. (5 authors)
records.abstract: The precatalytic spliceosome (B complex) is preceded by the pre-B complex. Here we report the cryo-electron microscopy structures of the Saccharomyces cerevisiae pre-B and B complexes at average resolutions of 3.3 to 4.6 and 3.9 angstroms, respectively. In the pre-B complex, the duplex between the 5' splice site (5'SS) and U1 small nuclear RNA (snRNA) is recognized by Yhc1, Luc7, and the Sm ring. In the B complex, U1 small nuclear ribonucleoprotein is dissociated, the 5'-exon-5'SS sequences are translocated near U6 snRNA, and three B-specific proteins may orient the precursor messenger RNA. In both complexes, U6 snRNA is anchored to loop I of U5 snRNA, and the duplex between the branch point sequence and U2 snRNA is recognized by the SF3b complex. Structural analysis reveals the mechanism of assembly and activation for the yeast spliceosome.

records.authors: Zhan X; Yan C; Zhang X; Lei J; Shi Y
records.authors_short: Zhan X; Yan C; Zhang X; et al. (5 authors)
records.abstract: The pre-catalytic spliceosome (B complex) is preceded by its precursor spliceosome (pre-B complex) and followed by the activated spliceosome (Bact complex). The pre-B-to-B and B-to-Bact transitions are driven by the ATPase/helicases Prp28 and Brr2, respectively. In this study, we report the cryo-electron microscopy structures of the human pre-B complex and the human B complex at an average resolution of 5.7 and 3.8 Å, respectively. In the pre-B complex, U1 and U2 small nuclear ribonucleoproteins (snRNPs) associate with two edges of the tetrahedron-shaped U4/U6.U5 tri-snRNP. The pre-mRNA is yet to be recognized by U5 or U6 small nuclear RNA (snRNA), and loop I of U5 snRNA remains unengaged. In the B complex, U1 snRNP and Prp28 are dissociated, the 5'-exon is anchored to loop I of U5 snRNA, and the 5'-splice site is recognized by U6 snRNA through duplex formation. In sharp contrast to S. cerevisiae, most components of U2 snRNP and tri-snRNP, exemplified by Brr2, undergo pronounced rearrangements in the human pre-B-to-B transition. Structural analysis reveals mechanistic insights into the assembly and activation of the human spliceosome.

records.title: Structure of a human fully-assembled precatalytic spliceosome (pre-B complex).
records.paper_title: Mechanism of 5' splice site transfer for human spliceosome activation.
records.authors: Charenton C; Wilkinson ME; Nagai K
records.authors_short: Charenton C; Wilkinson ME; Nagai K
records.abstract: The prespliceosome, comprising U1 and U2 small nuclear ribonucleoproteins (snRNPs) bound to the precursor messenger RNA 5' splice site (5'SS) and branch point sequence, associates with the U4/U6.U5 tri-snRNP to form the fully assembled precatalytic pre-B spliceosome. Here, we report cryo-electron microscopy structures of the human pre-B complex captured before U1 snRNP dissociation at 3.3-angstrom core resolution and the human tri-snRNP at 2.9-angstrom resolution. U1 snRNP inserts the 5'SS-U1 snRNA helix between the two RecA domains of the Prp28 DEAD-box helicase. Adenosine 5'-triphosphate-dependent closure of the Prp28 RecA domains releases the 5'SS to pair with the nearby U6 ACAGAGA-box sequence presented as a mobile loop. The structures suggest that formation of the 5'SS-ACAGAGA helix triggers remodeling of an intricate protein-RNA network to induce Brr2 helicase relocation to its loading sequence in U4 snRNA, enabling Brr2 to unwind the U4/U6 snRNA duplex to allow U6 snRNA to form the catalytic center of the spliceosome.

records.title: Cryo-EM structure of human exon-defined spliceosome in the late pre-B state.
records.paper_title: Structural insights into human exon-defined spliceosome prior to activation.
records.authors: Zhang W; Zhang X; Zhan X; Bai R; Lei J; Yan C; Shi Y
records.authors_short: Zhang W; Zhang X; Zhan X; et al. (7 authors)
records.abstract: Spliceosome is often assembled across an exon and undergoes rearrangement to span a neighboring intron. Most states of the intron-defined spliceosome have been structurally characterized. However, the structure of a fully assembled exon-defined spliceosome remains at large. During spliceosome assembly, the pre-catalytic state (B complex) is converted from its precursor (pre-B complex). Here we report atomic structures of the exon-defined human spliceosome in four sequential states: mature pre-B, late pre-B, early B, and mature B. In the previously unknown late pre-B state, U1 snRNP is already released but the remaining proteins are still in the pre-B state; unexpectedly, the RNAs are in the B state, with U6 snRNA forming a duplex with 5'-splice site and U5 snRNA recognizing the 3'-end of the exon. In the early and mature B complexes, the B-specific factors are stepwise recruited and specifically recognize the exon 3'-region. Our study reveals key insights into the assembly of the exon-defined spliceosomes and identifies mechanistic steps of the pre-B-to-B transition.

records.title: Cryo-EM Structure of Pre-B+5'ss+ATPgammaS Complex (core part)
records.paper_title: Structural insights into the cross-exon to cross-intron spliceosome switch.
records.authors: Zhang Z; Kumar V; Dybkov O; Will CL; Zhong J; Ludwig SEJ; Urlaub H; Kastner B; Stark H; Lührmann R
records.authors_short: Zhang Z; Kumar V; Dybkov O; et al. (10 authors)
records.abstract: Early spliceosome assembly can occur through an intron-defined pathway, whereby U1 and U2 small nuclear ribonucleoprotein particles (snRNPs) assemble across the intron1. Alternatively, it can occur through an exon-defined pathway2-5, whereby U2 binds the branch site located upstream of the defined exon and U1 snRNP interacts with the 5' splice site located directly downstream of it. The U4/U6.U5 tri-snRNP subsequently binds to produce a cross-intron (CI) or cross-exon (CE) pre-B complex, which is then converted to the spliceosomal B complex6,7. Exon definition promotes the splicing of upstream introns2,8,9 and plays a key part in alternative splicing regulation10-16. However, the three-dimensional structure of exon-defined spliceosomal complexes and the molecular mechanism of the conversion from a CE-organized to a CI-organized spliceosome, a pre-requisite for splicing catalysis, remain poorly understood. Here cryo-electron microscopy analyses of human CE pre-B complex and B-like complexes reveal extensive structural similarities with their CI counterparts. The results indicate that the CE and CI spliceosome assembly pathways converge already at the pre-B stage. Add-back experiments using purified CE pre-B complexes, coupled with cryo-electron microscopy, elucidate the order of the extensive remodelling events that accompany the formation of B complexes and B-like complexes. The molecular triggers and roles of B-specific proteins in these rearrangements are also identified. We show that CE pre-B complexes can productively bind in trans to a U1 snRNP-bound 5' splice site. Together, our studies provide new mechanistic insights into the CE to CI switch during spliceosome assembly and its effect on pre-mRNA splice site pairing at this stage.

The U4/U6.U5 tri-snRNP subsequently binds to produce a cross-intron (CI) or cross-exon (CE) pre-B complex, which is then converted to the spliceosomal B complex6,7. Exon definition promotes the splicing of upstream introns2,8,9 and plays a key part in alternative splicing regulation10-16. However, the three-dimensional structure of exon-defined spliceosomal complexes and the molecular mechanism of the conversion from a CE-organized to a CI-organized spliceosome, a pre-requisite for splicing catalysis, remain poorly understood. Here cryo-electron microscopy analyses of human CE pre-B complex and B-like complexes reveal extensive structural similarities with their CI counterparts. The results indicate that the CE and CI spliceosome assembly pathways converge already at the pre-B stage. Add-back experiments using purified CE pre-B complexes, coupled with cryo-electron microscopy, elucidate the order of the extensive remodelling events that accompany the formation of B complexes and B-like complexes. The molecular triggers and roles of B-specific proteins in these rearrangements are also identified. We show that CE pre-B complexes can productively bind in trans to a U1 snRNP-bound 5' splice site. Together, our studies provide new mechanistic insights into the CE to CI switch during spliceosome assembly and its effect on pre-mRNA splice site pairing at this stage.

records.title: Cryo-EM Structure of the human minor pre-B complex (pre-precatalytic spliceosome) U11 and tri-snRNP part
records.paper_title: Structural basis of U12-type intron engagement by the fully assembled human minor spliceosome.
records.authors: Bai R; Yuan M; Zhang P; Luo T; Shi Y; Wan R
records.authors_short: Bai R; Yuan M; Zhang P; et al. (6 authors)
records.abstract: The minor spliceosome, which is responsible for the splicing of U12-type introns, comprises five small nuclear RNAs (snRNAs), of which only one is shared with the major spliceosome. In this work, we report the 3.3-angstrom cryo-electron microscopy structure of the fully assembled human minor spliceosome pre-B complex. The atomic model includes U11 small nuclear ribonucleoprotein (snRNP), U12 snRNP, and U4atac/U6atac.U5 tri-snRNP. U11 snRNA is recognized by five U11-specific proteins (20K, 25K, 35K, 48K, and 59K) and the heptameric Sm ring. The 3' half of the 5'-splice site forms a duplex with U11 snRNA; the 5' half is recognized by U11-35K, U11-48K, and U11 snRNA. Two proteins, CENATAC and DIM2/TXNL4B, specifically associate with the minor tri-snRNP. A structural analysis uncovered how two conformationally similar tri-snRNPs are differentiated by the minor and major prespliceosomes for assembly.

records.title: Human pre-Bact-2 spliceosome core structure
records.paper_title: Mechanism of protein-guided folding of the active site U2/U6 RNA during spliceosome activation.
records.authors: Townsend C; Leelaram MN; Agafonov DE; Dybkov O; Will CL; Bertram K; Urlaub H; Kastner B; Stark H; Lührmann R
records.authors_short: Townsend C; Leelaram MN; Agafonov DE; et al. (10 authors)
records.abstract: Spliceosome activation involves extensive protein and RNA rearrangements that lead to formation of a catalytically active U2/U6 RNA structure. At present, little is known about the assembly pathway of the latter and the mechanism whereby proteins aid its proper folding. Here, we report the cryo-electron microscopy structures of two human, activated spliceosome precursors (that is, pre-Bact complexes) at core resolutions of 3.9 and 4.2 angstroms. These structures elucidate the order of the numerous protein exchanges that occur during activation, the mutually exclusive interactions that ensure the correct order of ribonucleoprotein rearrangements needed to form the U2/U6 catalytic RNA, and the stepwise folding pathway of the latter. Structural comparisons with mature Bact complexes reveal the molecular mechanism whereby a conformational change in the scaffold protein PRP8 facilitates final three-dimensional folding of the U2/U6 catalytic RNA.

records.title: The cryo-EM structure of human pre-Bact complex
records.paper_title: Molecular basis for the activation of human spliceosome.
records.authors: Zhan X; Lu Y; Shi Y
records.authors_short: Zhan X; Lu Y; Shi Y
records.abstract: The spliceosome executes pre-mRNA splicing through four sequential stages: assembly, activation, catalysis, and disassembly. Activation of the spliceosome, namely remodeling of the pre-catalytic spliceosome (B complex) into the activated spliceosome (Bact complex) and the catalytically activated spliceosome (B* complex), involves major flux of protein components and structural rearrangements. Relying on a splicing inhibitor, we have captured six intermediate states between the B and B* complexes: pre-Bact, Bact-I, Bact-II, Bact-III, Bact-IV, and post-Bact. Their cryo-EM structures, together with an improved structure of the catalytic step I spliceosome (C complex), reveal how the catalytic center matures around the internal stem loop of U6 snRNA, how the branch site approaches 5'-splice site, how the RNA helicase PRP2 rearranges to bind pre-mRNA, and how U2 snRNP undergoes remarkable movement to facilitate activation. We identify a previously unrecognized key role of PRP2 in spliceosome activation. Our study recapitulates a molecular choreography of the human spliceosome during its catalytic activation.

primary_map_scripts.3jb9:
# Lightweight ChimeraX script for 3JB9
# Models are downloaded directly by ChimeraX from the PDB.
# Primary maps, when requested, are downloaded directly from EMDB.
# Original deposited chain identifiers are used.

# Shared anchor frame: 8H6L
# Structure group: #301; target model: #301.1; RNA labels: #301.2.

open 8h6l id 301.9 name 8H6L_anchor
open 3jb9 id 301.1 name 3JB9
open emdb:6413 id 301.3.1 name 3JB9_EMD-6413_primary_map
rename #301 ILS_3jb9_structure

# Align on PRPF8.
matchmaker #301.1/A to #301.9/5B bring #301.3.1
close #301.9

hide atoms
cartoon
# Default display style.
style (protein|nucleic|solvent) & @@draw_mode=0 stick
cartoon style modeh def arrows t arrowshelix f arrowscale 3 wid 4 thick 3 sides 12 div 20
cartoon style ~(nucleic|strand) x round
cartoon style (nucleic|strand) x rect
cartoon style nucleic x round width 4 thick 4
lighting shadows false
lighting simple
camera ortho
lighting soft intensity 0.2 fillIntensity 0.5 ambientIntensity 1
lighting soft
# window
# windowsize 800 800

# bg and model colors
set bgColor white
style nucleic stick
graphics silhouettes true
cartoon style arrowScale 1.5
color byhetero
# Subunit colors from color_code/subunit_color_code.csv.
# PRPF8
color #301.1/A #BFC3E8
# U5-Sm SNRPG
color #301.1/J #B0C4DE
# U2-Sm SNRPG
color #301.1/o #D5F5E3
# DDX46
color #301.1/K #5BAE8A
# PRP8BP
color #301.1/L #E2F3F4
# SNW1
color #301.1/M #D49F96
# U6 snRNA
color #301.1/N #DC143C
# pre-mRNA
color #301.1/O #303030
# pre-mRNA
color #301.1/Q #303030

color #301.1/P #006400

primary_map_scripts.5zya:
# Lightweight ChimeraX script for 5ZYA
# Models are downloaded directly by ChimeraX from the PDB.
# Primary maps, when requested, are downloaded directly from EMDB.
# Original deposited chain identifiers are used.

# Shared anchor frame: 8H6L
# Structure group: #329; target model: #329.1; RNA labels: #329.2.

open 8h6l id 329.9 name 8H6L_anchor
open 5zya id 329.1 name 5ZYA
open emdb:6915 id 329.3.1 name 5ZYA_EMD-6915_primary_map
rename #329 SF3B_U2_5zya_structure

# Align on SF3B1.
matchmaker #329.1/C to #329.9/2G bring #329.3.1
close #329.9

hide atoms
cartoon
# Default display style.
style (protein|nucleic|solvent) & @@draw_mode=0 stick
cartoon style modeh def arrows t arrowshelix f arrowscale 3 wid 4 thick 3 sides 12 div 20
cartoon style ~(nucleic|strand) x round
cartoon style (nucleic|strand) x rect
cartoon style nucleic x round width 4 thick 4
lighting shadows false
lighting simple
camera ortho
lighting soft intensity 0.2 fillIntensity 0.5 ambientIntensity 1
lighting soft

set bgColor white
style nucleic stick
graphics silhouettes true
cartoon style arrowScale 1.5
color byhetero

# SF3B1
color #329.1/C #487957
# PHF5A
color #329.1/D #375A40
# SF3B3
color #329.1/A #6A9064
view
show nucleic

primary_map_scripts.6j6g:
# Lightweight ChimeraX script for 6J6G
# Models are downloaded directly by ChimeraX from the PDB.
# Primary maps, when requested, are downloaded directly from EMDB.
# Original deposited chain identifiers are used.

# Shared anchor frame: 8H6L
# Structure group: #340; target model: #340.1; RNA labels: #340.2.

open 8h6l id 340.9 name 8H6L_anchor
open 6j6g id 340.1 name 6J6G
open emdb:0686 id 340.3.1 name 6J6G_EMD-0686_primary_map
rename #340 Bstar_6j6g_structure

# Align on PRPF8.
matchmaker #340.1/A to #340.9/5B bring #340.3.1
close #340.9

hide atoms
cartoon
# Default display style.
style (protein|nucleic|solvent) & @@draw_mode=0 stick
cartoon style modeh def arrows t arrowshelix f arrowscale 3 wid 4 thick 3 sides 12 div 20

cartoon style (nucleic|strand) x rect

primary_map_scripts.6y50:
# Lightweight ChimeraX script for 6Y50
# Models are downloaded directly by ChimeraX from the PDB.
# Primary maps, when requested, are downloaded directly from EMDB.
# Original deposited chain identifiers are used.

# Shared anchor frame: 8H6L
# Structure group: #349; target model: #349.1; RNA labels: #349.2.

open 8h6l id 349.9 name 8H6L_anchor
open 6y50 id 349.1 name 6Y50
open emdb:10688 id 349.3.1 name 6Y50_EMD-10688_primary_map
rename #349 SF3B_U2_6y50_structure

# Align on SF3B1.
matchmaker #349.1/u to #349.9/2G bring #349.3.1
close #349.9

hide atoms
cartoon
# Default display style.
style (protein|nucleic|solvent) & @@draw_mode=0 stick
cartoon style modeh def arrows t arrowshelix f arrowscale 3 wid 4 thick 3 sides 12 div 20
cartoon style ~(nucleic|strand) x round
cartoon style (nucleic|strand) x rect
cartoon style nucleic x round width 4 thick 4
lighting shadows false
lighting simple
camera ortho
lighting soft intensity 0.2 fillIntensity 0.5 ambientIntensity 1
lighting soft
# window
# windowsize 800 800

# bg and model colors
set bgColor white
style nucleic stick
graphics silhouettes true
cartoon style arrowScale 1.5
color byhetero

primary_map_scripts.8i0t:

# Primary maps, when requested, are downloaded directly from EMDB.

# Shared anchor frame: 8H6L

open 8h6l id 387.9 name 8H6L_anchor
open 8i0t id 387.1 name 8I0T
open emdb:35109 id 387.3.1 name 8I0T_EMD-35109_primary_map
rename #387 Bact_8i0t_structure

primary_map_scripts.8qoz:
# Lightweight ChimeraX script for 8QOZ
# Models are downloaded directly by ChimeraX from the PDB.
# Primary maps, when requested, are downloaded directly from EMDB.
# Original deposited chain identifiers are used.

# Shared anchor frame: 8H6L
# Structure group: #398; target model: #398.1; RNA labels: #398.2.

open 8h6l id 398.9 name 8H6L_anchor
open 8qoz id 398.1 name 8QOZ
open emdb:18542 id 398.3.1 name 8QOZ_EMD-18542_primary_map
rename #398 pre_B_8qoz_structure

# Align on PRPF8.
matchmaker #398.1/A to #398.9/5B bring #398.3.1
close #398.9

hide atoms
cartoon
# Default display style.
style (protein|nucleic|solvent) & @@draw_mode=0 stick
cartoon style modeh def arrows t arrowshelix f arrowscale 3 wid 4 thick 3 sides 12 div 20
cartoon style ~(nucleic|strand) x round
cartoon style (nucleic|strand) x rect
cartoon style nucleic x round width 4 thick 4
lighting shadows false
lighting simple
camera ortho
lighting soft intensity 0.2 fillIntensity 0.5 ambientIntensity 1
lighting soft

primary_map_scripts.8rc0:
# Lightweight ChimeraX script for 8RC0
# Models are downloaded directly by ChimeraX from the PDB.
# Primary maps, when requested, are downloaded directly from EMDB.
# Original deposited chain identifiers are used.

# Shared anchor frame: 8H6L
# Structure group: #411; target model: #411.1; RNA labels: #411.2.

open 8h6l id 411.9 name 8H6L_anchor
open 8rc0 id 411.1 name 8RC0
open emdb:19041 id 411.3.1 name 8RC0_EMD-19041_primary_map
rename #411 tri_snRNP_8rc0_structure

# Align on PRPF8.
matchmaker #411.1/A to #411.9/5B bring #411.3.1
close #411.9

hide atoms
cartoon
# Default display style.
style (protein|nucleic|solvent) & @@draw_mode=0 stick
cartoon style modeh def arrows t arrowshelix f arrowscale 3 wid 4 thick 3 sides 12 div 20
cartoon style ~(nucleic|strand) x round
cartoon style (nucleic|strand) x rect
cartoon style nucleic x round width 4 thick 4
lighting shadows false
lighting simple
camera ortho
lighting soft intensity 0.2 fillIntensity 0.5 ambientIntensity 1
lighting soft
# window
# windowsize 800 800

set bgColor white
style nucleic stick
graphics silhouettes true
cartoon style arrowScale 1.5
color byhetero

primary_map_scripts.9k1o:
# Lightweight ChimeraX script for 9K1O
# Models are downloaded directly by ChimeraX from the PDB.
# Primary maps, when requested, are downloaded directly from EMDB.
# Original deposited chain identifiers are used.

open 8h6l id 420.9 name 8H6L_anchor
open 9k1o id 420.1 name 9K1O
open emdb:61977 id 420.3.1 name 9K1O_EMD-61977_primary_map
rename #420 SF3B_U2_9k1o_structure

# Align on SF3B1.
matchmaker #420.1/C to #420.9/2G bring #420.3.1
close #420.9

hide atoms
cartoon
# Default display style.
style (protein|nucleic|solvent) & @@draw_mode=0 stick

lighting shadows false

style nucleic stick

rna_feature_definitions.label: substrate RNA
rna_feature_definitions.definition: Complete deposited pre-mRNA, mRNA, exon, intron, or splice-site oligo entity used as substrate.
rna_feature_definitions.notes: Resolved residues only are selected in ChimeraX.

rna_feature_definitions.label: 5' splice site
rna_feature_definitions.definition: Intron 5' boundary and donor sequence, typically GU-containing and paired by U1 or U6.
rna_feature_definitions.notes: Defines the start of intron and end of 5' exon when present.

rna_feature_definitions.label: 5' exon
rna_feature_definitions.definition: Substrate residues upstream of the annotated 5' splice site.
rna_feature_definitions.notes: Can be inferred from the 5' splice site alone.

rna_feature_definitions.label: exon-defined exon
rna_feature_definitions.definition: Internal exon in exon-defined substrates, bounded by an upstream 3' splice site and a downstream 5' splice site.
rna_feature_definitions.notes: Used for exon-defined or cross-exon spliceosomes so upstream intron residues are not mislabeled as a 5' exon.

rna_feature_definitions.label: intron
rna_feature_definitions.definition: Substrate intron region beginning at the 5' splice site, or the upstream intron ending at the 3' splice site in exon-defined substrates.
rna_feature_definitions.notes: If one splice-site boundary is not identified, the displayed selection covers only resolved intron-side residues with available boundary support.

rna_feature_definitions.label: branch point region
rna_feature_definitions.definition: Branch-point sequence around the reactive branch adenosine.
rna_feature_definitions.notes: Candidate motifs are scored against state-specific RNA-network expectations, U2 branch-region base pairs, U2 proximity, motif strength, and residue-numbering continuity.

rna_feature_definitions.label: branch point adenosine
rna_feature_definitions.definition: Reactive adenosine that attacks the 5' splice site during first-step catalysis.
rna_feature_definitions.notes: Selected as one residue when resolved; network-supported calls are preferred over first motif matches.

rna_feature_definitions.label: polypyrimidine tract
rna_feature_definitions.definition: C/U-rich region upstream of the 3' splice site in many metazoan substrates.
rna_feature_definitions.notes: Only emitted when motif and resolved residues are present.

rna_feature_definitions.label: 3' splice site
rna_feature_definitions.definition: Acceptor YAG sequence at the intron 3' boundary.
rna_feature_definitions.notes: Current scanner is conservative and may not annotate every engineered substrate.

rna_feature_definitions.label: 3' exon
rna_feature_definitions.definition: Substrate residues downstream of the annotated 3' splice site.
rna_feature_definitions.notes: Requires a 3' splice-site assignment.

rna_feature_definitions.label: ligated exons
rna_feature_definitions.definition: Spliced exon product after exon ligation.
rna_feature_definitions.notes: Usually comes from deposited entity names.

rna_feature_definitions.label: intron lariat
rna_feature_definitions.definition: Excised branched intron product.
rna_feature_definitions.notes: Usually comes from deposited entity names.

rna_feature_definitions.label: U1 5' splice-site recognition
rna_feature_definitions.definition: 5' end of U1/U11 snRNA that base-pairs with the 5' splice site.
rna_feature_definitions.notes: Approximate review-derived interval; selected only where resolved.

rna_feature_definitions.label: U11 5' splice-site recognition
rna_feature_definitions.definition: 5' end of U11 snRNA that base-pairs with U12-type 5' splice sites.
rna_feature_definitions.notes: Human minor spliceosome interval from Ding et al. WIREs RNA 2022 and deposited minor structures.

rna_feature_definitions.label: U2 branchpoint pairing region
rna_feature_definitions.definition: U2/U12 snRNA region pairing with the branch site while bulging the branch adenosine.
rna_feature_definitions.notes: Motif-neighborhood based when possible so adjacent base-paired residues are included.

rna_feature_definitions.label: U12 branchpoint pairing region
rna_feature_definitions.definition: U12 snRNA region pairing with the conserved U12-type branch site while bulging the branch adenosine.
rna_feature_definitions.notes: Human U12 reference nt 16-26; validated where R2DT base pairs to substrate are present.

rna_feature_definitions.label: U12/U6atac helix I partner
rna_feature_definitions.definition: U12-side sequence paired with U6atac in activated minor spliceosomes.
rna_feature_definitions.notes: Human U12 reference nt 2-11 from minor-spliceosome structural base pairs.

rna_feature_definitions.label: U2/U6 helix I partner
rna_feature_definitions.definition: U2-side sequence contributing to U2/U6 helix I.
rna_feature_definitions.notes: Approximate interval for cross-structure selection.

rna_feature_definitions.label: U2 stem IIa
rna_feature_definitions.definition: U2 snRNA stem IIa region implicated in spliceosome activation.
rna_feature_definitions.notes: Approximate interval for cross-structure selection.

rna_feature_definitions.label: U2 Sm site
rna_feature_definitions.definition: Sm-binding site of U2/U12 snRNA.
rna_feature_definitions.notes: AUUUUUG-like motif.

rna_feature_definitions.label: U4/U6 stem I partner
rna_feature_definitions.definition: U4/U4atac-side sequence paired with U6/U6atac in U4/U6 di-snRNP.
rna_feature_definitions.notes: Reference-aligned for human and budding yeast where possible.

rna_feature_definitions.label: U4/U6 stem II partner
rna_feature_definitions.definition: U4/U4atac-side sequence paired with U6/U6atac stem II.
rna_feature_definitions.notes: Reference-aligned for human and budding yeast where possible.

rna_feature_definitions.label: U4atac/U6atac stem I partner
rna_feature_definitions.definition: U4atac-side sequence paired with U6atac in minor tri-snRNP states.
rna_feature_definitions.notes: Human U4atac reference aligned; modified leading residues are tolerated.

rna_feature_definitions.label: U4atac/U6atac stem II partner
rna_feature_definitions.definition: U4atac-side sequence paired with U6atac stem II in minor tri-snRNP states.
rna_feature_definitions.notes: Human U4atac reference aligned; selected only where resolved.

rna_feature_definitions.label: U4atac Brr2 loading region
rna_feature_definitions.definition: Approximate U4atac region near Brr2 engagement in minor tri-snRNP structures.
rna_feature_definitions.notes: Approximate interval.

rna_feature_definitions.label: U4 Brr2 loading region
rna_feature_definitions.definition: U4 region engaged by Brr2 during U4/U6 unwinding.
rna_feature_definitions.notes: Approximate interval.

rna_feature_definitions.label: U4 Sm site
rna_feature_definitions.definition: Sm-binding site of U4/U4atac snRNA.
rna_feature_definitions.notes: AUUUUUG-like motif.

rna_feature_definitions.label: U5 loop I
rna_feature_definitions.definition: Conserved U5 loop contacting exon termini.
rna_feature_definitions.notes: Restricted to expected loop-I interval to avoid off-target CCUU motifs.

rna_feature_definitions.label: U5 loop I 5' exon contact side
rna_feature_definitions.definition: Approximate U5 loop-I side contacting the 5' exon.
rna_feature_definitions.notes: Approximate interval.

rna_feature_definitions.label: U5 loop I 3' exon contact side
rna_feature_definitions.definition: Approximate U5 loop-I side contacting the 3' exon.
rna_feature_definitions.notes: Approximate interval.

rna_feature_definitions.label: U5 Sm site
rna_feature_definitions.definition: Sm-binding site of U5 snRNA.
rna_feature_definitions.notes: AUUUUUG-like motif.

rna_feature_definitions.label: U6 ACAGAGA box
rna_feature_definitions.definition: Conserved U6/U6atac ACAGAGA element interacting with the 5' splice site.
rna_feature_definitions.notes: High confidence when resolved.

rna_feature_definitions.label: U6atac 5' splice-site recognition
rna_feature_definitions.definition: U6atac 5'SS-recognition region contacting the U12-type 5' splice site after activation.
rna_feature_definitions.notes: Human U6atac reference nt 11-17 around the AAGGA motif.

rna_feature_definitions.label: U6atac/U12 helix I partner
rna_feature_definitions.definition: U6atac-side sequence paired with U12 in activated minor spliceosomes.
rna_feature_definitions.notes: Human U6atac reference nt 23-29 from structural base pairs.

rna_feature_definitions.label: U6 5'SS upstream contact
rna_feature_definitions.definition: Neighborhood around the ACAGAGA box that contacts the 5' splice site.
rna_feature_definitions.notes: Derived from ACAGAGA position.

rna_feature_definitions.label: U6/U2 helix I partner
rna_feature_definitions.definition: U6-side sequence contributing to U2/U6 helix I.
rna_feature_definitions.notes: Approximate interval.

rna_feature_definitions.label: U6 5' terminal stem-loop
rna_feature_definitions.definition: 5' terminal intramolecular stem-loop of U6 snRNA.
rna_feature_definitions.notes: Separate from U4/U6 stem I; reference-aligned for human and budding yeast.

rna_feature_definitions.label: U6/U4 stem I partner
rna_feature_definitions.definition: U6-side sequence paired with U4 before activation.
rna_feature_definitions.notes: Emitted only when U4/U4atac is present in the same structure.

rna_feature_definitions.label: U6/U4 stem II partner
rna_feature_definitions.definition: U6-side sequence paired with U4/U4atac stem II before activation.
rna_feature_definitions.notes: Emitted only when U4/U4atac is present in the same structure.

rna_feature_definitions.label: U6 internal stem-loop
rna_feature_definitions.definition: U6 internal stem-loop near the catalytic metal-binding region.
rna_feature_definitions.notes: Approximate interval around AGC triad.

rna_feature_definitions.label: U6 AGC catalytic triad
rna_feature_definitions.definition: Conserved AGC triad in the catalytic U6 ISL region.
rna_feature_definitions.notes: Medium confidence motif call.

rna_feature_definitions.label: U6 LSm site
rna_feature_definitions.definition: 3' terminal LSm-binding region of U6/U6atac.
rna_feature_definitions.notes: Approximate terminal interval.

rna_elements.name: 5' splice site
rna_elements.function: Defines the exon-intron boundary and is first recognized by U1 snRNP, then transferred to U6 during activation.
rna_elements.interactors: U1 5' end in E/A complex; U6 ACAGAGA/5'SS-contact region after activation; U5 loop I aligns the exon near this site.
rna_elements.remodeling: U1 is displaced during transition to Bact, allowing U6 to engage the 5'SS.
rna_elements.helicases: Brr2/SNRNP200 drives U4/U6 unwinding; Prp28/DDX23 promotes U1-5'SS release in many systems.

rna_elements.name: Branch point adenosine
rna_elements.function: The nucleophile for the first catalytic step; it attacks the 5'SS to form the intron lariat.
rna_elements.interactors: Initially sequestered by SF3B around the branch helix; base-pairs with the U2 branchpoint-pairing region while the BP-A remains bulged.
rna_elements.remodeling: Activation and SF3B remodeling expose the branch helix to the catalytic core.
rna_elements.helicases: Prp2/DHX16 promotes branch-region remodeling before catalysis.

rna_elements.name: Polypyrimidine tract and 3' splice site
rna_elements.function: Helps define the downstream splice acceptor and positions the second-step substrate.
rna_elements.interactors: Recognized by early assembly factors and later positioned near Prp16/Prp22-dependent catalytic conformations.
rna_elements.remodeling: The 3'SS is repositioned after the first step so the 5' exon can attack it in exon ligation.
rna_elements.helicases: Prp16/DHX38 remodels the spliceosome after branching; Prp22/DHX8 acts around exon ligation and mRNA release.

rna_elements.name: 5' exon and 3' exon
rna_elements.function: The exons are held near U5 loop I for alignment before exon ligation.
rna_elements.interactors: U5 loop I contacts exon nucleotides; Prp8/PRPF8 forms the central active-site scaffold.
rna_elements.remodeling: After branching, the exon ends are repositioned for the second catalytic step.
rna_elements.helicases: Prp16/DHX38 and Prp22/DHX8 contribute to second-step remodeling and mRNA release.

rna_elements.name: U1 5'SS-recognition region
rna_elements.function: Early recognition of the 5' splice site in E/A complexes.
rna_elements.interactors: Base-pairs with the 5'SS; replaced by U6 during activation.
rna_elements.helicases: Prp28/DDX23 promotes U1 release from the 5'SS.

rna_elements.name: U2 branchpoint-pairing region
rna_elements.function: Forms the branch helix with the intron branch-site sequence while leaving the BP-A bulged.
rna_elements.interactors: Substrate branch region, SF3B complex, and later the U6 catalytic core.
rna_elements.remodeling: SF3B and branch helix positioning change during transition from A/pre-B to activated states.
rna_elements.helicases: Prp2/DHX16 remodels branch-region contacts before first-step catalysis.

rna_elements.name: U4/U6 stems I and II
rna_elements.function: Hold U6 in an inactive duplex within the tri-snRNP before activation.
rna_elements.interactors: U4 and U6 snRNAs, Prp3/PRPF3, Prp4/PRPF4, Prp31/PRPF31, and Brr2/SNRNP200.
rna_elements.remodeling: Unwound during activation, freeing U6 to pair with U2 and form catalytic structures.

rna_elements.name: U2/U6 helix I
rna_elements.function: Major catalytic RNA-RNA interaction formed after U4/U6 unwinding.
rna_elements.interactors: U2 and U6 snRNAs; coordinates with the U6 ISL and ACAGAGA region in the active site.
rna_elements.remodeling: Forms during activation and is later dismantled during disassembly/recycling.
rna_elements.helicases: Brr2/SNRNP200 enables formation indirectly by releasing U6 from U4; Prp43/DHX15 contributes to disassembly.

rna_elements.name: U6 ACAGAGA box
rna_elements.function: Conserved U6 region that helps dock and position the 5' splice site in the activated spliceosome.
rna_elements.interactors: 5'SS region of the substrate and the PRPF8 active-site environment.
rna_elements.remodeling: Becomes active after U1 release and U4/U6 unwinding.
rna_elements.helicases: Prp28/DDX23 and Brr2/SNRNP200 act upstream of productive ACAGAGA-5'SS engagement.

rna_elements.name: U6 internal stem loop and AGC triad
rna_elements.function: Core catalytic U6 elements that coordinate the active-site metal-ion environment.
rna_elements.interactors: U2/U6 catalytic RNA network, PRPF8, and splice-site substrates.
rna_elements.remodeling: Assembled during activation and rearranged through catalytic states.
rna_elements.helicases: Prp2/DHX16 and Prp16/DHX38 remodel active-site substrate conformations around these elements.

rna_elements.name: U5 loop I
rna_elements.function: Aligns exon sequences for both catalytic steps, especially exon ligation.
rna_elements.remodeling: Maintains exon contacts as the spliceosome moves from branching to exon ligation and mRNA release.
rna_elements.helicases: Prp16/DHX38 and Prp22/DHX8 act during second-step remodeling and product release.

protein_complexes.name: U1 snRNP
protein_complexes.leaves: Before or during activation to Bact
protein_complexes.role: Recognizes the 5' splice site and nucleates early spliceosome assembly.
protein_complexes.remodeling: U1-5'SS pairing is replaced by U6-5'SS engagement.
protein_complexes.key_factors: U1 snRNA, U1-70K, U1A, U1C, Sm ring; Prp28/DDX23 promotes release.

protein_complexes.name: U2 snRNP and SF3B
protein_complexes.leaves: Core U2 remains through catalysis; SF3B is remodeled/reduced in activated states
protein_complexes.role: Recognizes the branch region and forms the U2-branch helix.
protein_complexes.remodeling: Branch-site proteins are repositioned to expose the BP-A for catalysis.
protein_complexes.key_factors: U2 snRNA, SF3A, SF3B, U2A'/U2B'', Sm ring; Prp2/DHX16 promotes later branch-region remodeling.

protein_complexes.name: U4/U6.U5 tri-snRNP
protein_complexes.leaves: U4 and many tri-snRNP assembly factors leave during activation; U5 and U6 remain
protein_complexes.role: Brings U5 and U6 to the assembling spliceosome while U6 is held inactive by U4.
protein_complexes.remodeling: U4/U6 unwinding activates U6 and allows U2/U6 helix formation.
protein_complexes.key_factors: PRPF8/Prp8, SNRNP200/Brr2, EFTUD2/Snu114, PRPF6, PRPF3/4/31, LSm ring; Brr2/SNRNP200 is central.

protein_complexes.name: U5 snRNP
protein_complexes.joins: With tri-snRNP, retained through catalysis and recycling
protein_complexes.role: Provides PRPF8 scaffold and U5 loop I exon-alignment platform.
protein_complexes.remodeling: Retains exon contacts while the active site changes through C, C*, P and ILS states.
protein_complexes.key_factors: PRPF8/Prp8, SNRNP200/Brr2, EFTUD2/Snu114, PRPF6, TXNL4A, CD2BP2, U5 snRNA.

protein_complexes.name: NTC
protein_complexes.role: Stabilizes the activated spliceosome after U4 release and supports catalytic-core organization.
protein_complexes.remodeling: Locks in U2/U6 and active-site architecture after activation.
protein_complexes.key_factors: PRPF19, CDC5L, PLRG1, BCAS2, SPF27/BCAS2-related factors.

protein_complexes.name: NTR and activation/remodeling factors
protein_complexes.role: Drive ATP-dependent rearrangements that license catalysis and proofread substrates.
protein_complexes.remodeling: Prp2/DHX16 promotes first-step activation; Prp16/DHX38 remodels after branching; Prp22/DHX8 promotes mRNA release.
protein_complexes.key_factors: CWC25, Yju2/CCDC130, PRP2/DHX16, PRP16/DHX38, PRP22/DHX8, step-specific cofactors.

protein_complexes.name: EJC and mRNP factors
protein_complexes.role: Deposit or stabilize proteins on the spliced mRNA product.
protein_complexes.remodeling: Associated with exon ligation and product handoff.
protein_complexes.key_factors: EIF4A3, MAGOH, RBM8A/Y14, CASC3/MLN51, SR proteins depending on state/species.

protein_complexes.name: Disassembly and recycling factors
protein_complexes.leaves: After spliceosome dismantling and snRNP recycling
protein_complexes.role: Dismantle post-catalytic and intron-lariat spliceosomes and recycle snRNPs.
protein_complexes.remodeling: Release lariat intron and recycle U2/U5/U6 components.
protein_complexes.key_factors: Prp43/DHX15, TFIP11/Ntr1, GCFC/Ntr2, PAXBP1, GPATCH1, C19L1/C19L2, DHX35.

splicing_cycle.name: E complex
splicing_cycle.composition: U1 snRNP recognizes the 5' splice site while SF1/BBP and U2AF define the branch region, polypyrimidine tract, and 3' splice site.
splicing_cycle.transition: Prp5 and Sub2/UAP56 help convert this early recognition state into a U2-containing prespliceosome.
splicing_cycle.notes: U2 is not yet stably incorporated in the canonical E complex; this is why the cycle cartoon shows U1 and substrate, but not U2, at this stage.

splicing_cycle.name: A complex
splicing_cycle.composition: U2 snRNP joins and pairs with the branch site, with the branch-point adenosine bulged from the U2-branch helix.
splicing_cycle.transition: The preassembled U4/U6.U5 tri-snRNP is recruited to form pre-B.
splicing_cycle.notes: This is the first major stage in the diagram where U2 is shown as part of the assembling spliceosome.

splicing_cycle.name: pre-B complex
splicing_cycle.composition: U1, U2, U4/U6, and U5 are present; U6 remains sequestered by U4.
splicing_cycle.transition: Prp28/DDX23 promotes transfer of the 5' splice site from U1 to U6.
splicing_cycle.notes: The complex is fully assembled but not catalytically active.

splicing_cycle.name: B complex
splicing_cycle.composition: U2, U5, U4/U6, and the transferred 5' splice-site/U6 interaction are present after U1 release.
splicing_cycle.transition: Brr2/SNRNP200 unwinds U4/U6 to release U4 and enable U2/U6 catalytic RNA network formation.
splicing_cycle.notes: B complex precedes activation and still lacks the mature catalytic RNA core.

splicing_cycle.name: Bact complex
splicing_cycle.composition: U4 has left; U2/U6 and U5 form the activated RNA-protein core with NTC/NTR factors.
splicing_cycle.transition: Prp2/DHX16 remodels the branch region and licenses first-step catalysis.
splicing_cycle.notes: Bact and pre-Bact entries are separated in the database when depositions support that distinction.

splicing_cycle.name: B* and C complex
splicing_cycle.composition: The branch point is positioned for attack on the 5' splice site, producing the cleaved 5' exon and intron lariat intermediate.
splicing_cycle.transition: Prp16/DHX38 remodels the spliceosome after branching toward the second-step conformation.
splicing_cycle.notes: C complex denotes the post-branching, pre-exon-ligation catalytic state.

splicing_cycle.name: C* and P complex
splicing_cycle.composition: The 3' splice site and exons are positioned for exon ligation, yielding spliced mRNA and a lariat intron product.
splicing_cycle.transition: Prp22/DHX8 acts around exon ligation and mRNA release.
splicing_cycle.notes: P complex is post-catalytic and retains the intron lariat spliceosome before disassembly.

splicing_cycle.name: ILS, DIS, and recycling
splicing_cycle.composition: The intron-lariat spliceosome retains U2/U5/U6 components and disassembly factors before dismantling.
splicing_cycle.transition: Prp43/DHX15 with Ntr1/TFIP11 and Ntr2/GCFC promotes spliceosome disassembly and recycling.
splicing_cycle.notes: DIS and U5 recycling states are shown as separate bins when deposited structures capture those late states.
